Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

(3 comments)

It would be good to have a test case for extents_stats, since we have nothing accessing these stats (or offset_stats or extents_stats_per_process) in the tests scripts, and clearly it is possible that the stats can be broken (I'm even surprised that it has taken this long).

A couple of minor improvements if the patch is refreshed.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(3 comments)

Oh, fun:
Tiny write & fast read don't update stats.  That means a pretty good % of all reads are missed...

Easy to fix, but yet another reason we need a test.
Uploaded patch set 3.

Patch Set 3:

Additional fun fact:
Putting the stats tally code in to the VVP layer means that i/os had already been chunked by stripe size as we're going around cl_io_loop, so the maximum recorded extent size was stripe size, regardless of user i/o size...

Moral of the story:
Write tests. -_-

Patch Set 3:

> Additional fun fact:
 > Putting the stats tally code in to the VVP layer means that i/os
 > had already been chunked by stripe size as we're going around
 > cl_io_loop, so the maximum recorded extent size was stripe size,
 > regardless of user i/o size...

Strange, I didn't see that.  Maybe my testing was on 1-stripe files, so they didn't get split, but I definitely saw very large IOs (I was running "lfs find -type f -size -1G | xargs grep -a . > /dev/null").

parents.subject: LU-12343 osc: Fix dom handling in weight_ast

commit.subject: LU-12394 llite: Fix extents_stats
commit.message:
LU-12394 llite: Fix extents_stats

Patch 32517 from LU-8066 changed:
        (1 << LL_HIST_START << i)

To:

        BIT(LL_HIST_START << i)

But these are not equivalent because this changes the order
of operations.  The earlier one does the operations in this
order:
        (1 << LL_HIST_START) << i

The new one is this order:
        1 << (LL_HIST_START << i)

Which is quite different, as it's left shifting
LL_HIST_START directly, and LL_HIST_START is a number of
bits.

The goal is really just to start with BIT(LL_HIST_START)
and left shift by one (going from 4K, to 8K, etc) each
time, so just use:
        BIT(LL_HIST_START + i)

The result of this was that all i/os over 8K were placed in
the 4K-8K stat bucket, because the loop exited early.

Also add mmap'ed reads & writes to extents_stats.

Add test for extents_stats.

Fixes: adb5aca3d673 ("LU-8066 llite: Move all remaining procfs entries
                     to debugfs")

Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: Iab4dc097234d411601a18d501075df45791d1138
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/35075
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>

Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
